topic.what: Pre-execution detection of malicious instructions hidden inside skill artifacts
topic.how: RouteGuard uses response-conditioned attention and hidden-state alignment through reliability-gated late fusion on frozen backbone
topic.why_matters: Skill poisoning enables goal hijacking via instruction-competition inside legitimate-looking carriers, evading text-only filtering

characteristics.what_it_measures: F1 score on Skill-Inject channel slice
characteristics.context: Skill-Inject channel slice, most critical detection scenario

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of description attacks missed by lexical screening that are recovered
Magnitude of attention shift toward malicious skill span
Attention shift difference between description-channel and direct line-edit poisoning

novelty: Formulated skill poisoning as instruction-competition inside instruction-like carriers
Demonstrated attention hijacking as structured internal mechanism correlated with goal hijacking

open_problems: Extension to runtime prevention beyond pre-execution screening

Handling of adaptive attackers aware of attention-based detection
